75.reason: [Error: Should print the starting vertex 'ii', not the deleted vertex 'v'. This will output the wrong BFS sequence.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [This prints the deleted vertex 'v' instead of the current starting vertex 'ii'. Also, traversal should start from vertex 0, but this loop may start BFS from any unvisited non-deleted vertex. Since the graph is connected and 0 is not deleted, the first unvisited is 0, but printing 'v' here is wrong. It should print 'ii'. Moreover, this breaks the requirement to start from 0.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Incorrect output: should print 'ii' (current vertex) not 'v' (vertex to delete)]

111.reason: [Error: Variable 'i' is out of scope here; it was used in the previous loop and now holds the value 'vern', causing an out-of-bounds access. Should use 'v' instead of 'i'.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Error: Variable 'i' is out of scope here; it was used in the previous loop and now holds the value 'vern', causing an out-of-bounds access. Should use 'v' instead of 'i'.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Logic error: uses i which is now out of bounds (i = vern after previous loop), should be v.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Here, 'i' is used after the loop where 'i' reached 'vern', so Ga[i][j] accesses out-of-bounds memory (Ga[vern][j]), which is undefined behavior. It should be Ga[v][j] = 1000; but note that the row for vertex 'v' doesn't need to be cleared because during traversal we skip vertex 'v' anyway. However, the main issue is using 'i' instead of 'v'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Index error: uses 'i' which is equal to 'vern' after previous loop, should use 'v']

139.reason: [Error: Should print the starting vertex 'ii', not hardcoded '0'. This will output the wrong BFS sequence after deletion.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Error: Should print the starting vertex 'ii', not hardcoded '0'. This will output the wrong BFS sequence after deletion.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.1111111111111111 , reason:  [Error: Should print the current vertex 'ii', not hardcoded '0'. This will output the wrong BFS sequence after deletion.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Logical error: prints constant 0 instead of the current start vertex.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Hardcoding "0 " is incorrect. It should print the current vertex 'ii'. Since traversal must start from 0 and 0 is not deleted, the first 'ii' is 0, but printing "0 " regardless of 'ii' is wrong if by chance the BFS started elsewhere (though it shouldn't). More critically, if the graph after deletion is still connected and starts at 0, then only when ii==0 should it print 0. But this prints "0 " even if ii is not 0, which is wrong. Also, reusing 'ii' in the inner loop below causes corruption.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Hardcoded output: should print 'ii', not hardcoded "0"]
